review_text:
My first 1 star review goes to...the very deserving Circus Circus.  This review has everything to do with their employees and nothing to do with the amenities of the hotel and casino.

We all know how fun it is to relive your childhood upstairs where all the games and circus shows go down so my friends and I spent some time up there.  We sat down to play Circus Downs, the game where you roll a ball into holes to try to get your horse across the board, but there was a cheater amongst us.  He stole a ball..and used 2 to play.  The worker did nothing to stop him and even awarded that jerk a prize at the end.  Seriously?! Little kids are playing this game and you're going to cheat them from a fair game??  When I asked the employee to let us play another round, he just ignored me!  He didn't even apologize for the cheater or simply say no.  A friend and I decided to report this situation to security...which turned out to be no help AT ALL.  

Us: *tap* Excuse me.
Security biatch: Don't touch me.
Us: I need to report an incident. We were upstairs-
Security: *with LOTS of attitude* Upstairs? Upstairs where? Midway? 
Us: Um...upstairs where all the gam..
Security: Get to the point. What.Do.You.Want?
*Oh hellll no! At this point..I've had it up to HERE with her attitude!*
Us: If you're busy and you need to be somewhere, then I can talk to someone else.  Just point me to the direction of a manager.
Security: *throws back her jacket flap to reveal her "badge* Security.
Us: Tells her the story...blah blah 
Security: What? So this is about a teddy bear? 
Us: NO, this about ethics and the way the employee inappropriately handled the situation.
Security: How old are you?
Us:......Why does that matter?
Security: I have the right to ask.

WHAT A BEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEE!!! Really?! You're a security guard and you're going around riding on your high horse??  Are you even doing your job securing the premises? I wish I had gotten HER name so that I could report her!!

As you can guess, talking to the manager of midway was pointless.  He apologized for the "terrible predicament" we were in and how he understands "it sucks."  WHY ARE THEY TALKING TO US AS IF WE'RE 8 YEARS OLD?! 

adijgoiasjdgoiasdg CIRCUS CIRCUS YOU GUYS SUCK!!!
issue: Employee ignored cheating, allowed the cheater to win a prize, and failed to address the complaint or apologize
fixes: Deploy a mandatory incident‑reporting workflow in ServiceNow where any employee who observes cheating triggers an automatic ticket (with timestamped video evidence upload) that must be reviewed within 24 hours by the floor manager. | - Integrate a post‑interaction CSAT survey into Salesforce that includes a “Was your concern addressed?” question; if the score falls below 80 %, the case is routed instantly to the quality assurance team for follow‑up and a corrective‑action plan. | - Institute a quarterly “Cheating Accountability” KPI tracked via Power BI, measuring the number of cheating incidents logged per 10,000 interactions, with a target of ≤0.05% and a formal review board that sanctions repeat offenders. | - Schedule weekly 15‑minute role‑play drills on Zoom using recorded cheating scenarios pulled from the internal knowledge base, scored by a certified facilitator and tied to individual performance bonuses.

review_text:
My review pertains only to the Pet Boarding facilities.

Their "Hound Hotel" sounds/looks really good online, but in person it was a real let down.  The kennel is in a room off the parking garage, steps from the city sidewalk.  I was told it was by a security desk, but when we arrived, that whole tower was closed and unattended.  If somone were able to get into the room, they could get to your dog through the space at the top of the cage, even though the cage was locked.  The cameras are a nice feature, but are you gonna watch the dog 24/7?  Even if you saw someone doing something with your dog, it would take you 10 minutes to get there from the other tower.  We packed up and went to the Reno Plaza hotel, who allows pets in the room.

If you care for your pet, you would not use these facilities.
issue: The online description of the "Hound Hotel" is misleading and does not match the actual facility experience
fixes: Deploy a dynamic content audit workflow in Contentful (or similar CMS) that flags any “Hound Hotel” property page with mismatched photos or copy; schedule automated daily scans and require approval from the brand compliance team before publishing. | - Integrate an AI‑driven visual verification step using Clarifai’s image similarity model into the editorial review checklist so every new listing photo is cross‑referenced against the on‑site standard before going live. | - Launch a quarterly mystery‑guest program managed through Medallia Experience Management, assigning at least five guests per location to complete a standardized “Facility Accuracy Survey” that scores descriptions vs. reality; trigger corrective actions when scores fall below 90 %. | - Create a centralized “Property Truth Database” in Airtable linked to the e‑commerce catalog, where every attribute (amenities, room type, photos) must be verified by the operations manager via a signed off sheet before the data syncs to the website.

review_text:
Ahhh Circus Circus Reno, lets take a tour shall we?  Keep in mind we're not expecting much, this IS Reno, not Vegas.

Lets check in!  Go to the front desk and be greeted by the most somber and unpleasant reception desk staff I've encountered.  Pretty much everybody we talk to up there seem to hate their life and made sure to show it.  What a great vibe to start our trip!

Take a walk through the casino floor on the way to the room.  Take a look around.  The wait staff looks like they've been working there since 1975, jaw-jacked clientele super focused on their slot game nearing the end of their 72 hour meth bender (bet your ass they're staying in the room next to you!).  Outstanding!  Unless you're waiting for the tram to take you to the Sky Tower (which "sometimes" works), looks like you're walking about 5-10 minutes to your room carrying all your shit, probably should have just got a bellhop, but expect the same bad customer service, so we'll do it ourselves.

Arriving in the room, you'll be greeted by questionable bedding and furniture that looks like it's been sitting in the room since about the time that the wait staff was hired (circa 1975 or so... at least the TV was from the late 80s or early 90s).  You might even be lucky enough to find some dirty towels in the bathroom.  If you forgot your lighter in the car you're shit outta luck, since there's no matches to be found in the room.

Spend about 60 minutes downstairs and get stared at by creepy crackheads and ignored by wait staff.  Lets go get a drink at the bar!  Why does the bartender act like I'm unconvincing him be even being there?  There's no "Hey bud what can I get cha" here, just a blank stare and a nod acknowledging that you did, in fact, place an order.

...


Word to the wise:  Go ahead and stay at any of the other Casinos apart from the Circus Circus-Silver Legacy-El Dorado group.  I'd recommend paying a little extra and staying at the Peppermill or Atlantis (Reno's best "Vegas-like" experience), or if you have to stay downtown, go ahead and stay at the Sands Regency, at least the staff is more friendly and the rooms are bigger, for a cheaper price.  Clientele is slightly on the shady side as well, but it's Reno, I wouldn't expect anything more.
issue: Reception and front‑desk staff were unfriendly and displayed a negative attitude
fixes: Deploy a weekly “Mystery Guest” audit (using the Medallia Experience Management platform) where anonymous guests score reception friendliness on a 1‑10 scale; tie results to quarterly performance bonuses for staff meeting ≥ 8.0 average scores. | - Launch a mandatory 20‑minute microlearning module in Lessonly titled “Positive Greetings & Active Listening,” with built‑in scenario quizzes that require ≥ 90% pass before staff can clock in each shift. | - Install a real‑time guest sentiment dashboard in Power BI that pulls data from the hotel’s PMS (e.g., Opera) to flag any front‑desk interaction scoring ≤ 7 on post‑checkout surveys, prompting immediate manager follow‑up within 24 hours. | - Create a rotating “Shift Champion” schedule tracked in Asana, assigning one employee per day to lead a 5‑minute daily huddle focusing on three positive interactions each shift and recording completion for HR reporting.

fixes: Deploy IoT vibration sensors on all tram motors and integrate them with Siemens S7‑PLC condition monitoring software to trigger automatic shutdowns when threshold alerts are detected, preventing mechanical failures before they impact service. | - Establish a preventive maintenance schedule in Fiix CMMS that mandates weekly lubrication checks, quarterly gearbox inspections, and monthly brake pad replacements, with KPI tracking of “mean time between unscheduled outages” to keep reliability above 98 %. | - Install RFID‑enabled door locks and integrate them with the existing SCADA system so that any lock failure automatically generates a ServiceNow incident ticket and a 15‑minute SLA for on‑site repair. | - Create a real‑time passenger notification dashboard in Power BI that pulls live tram status data from the onboard GPS modules, displaying estimated arrival times and alternative routes, reducing perceived unreliability by over 20 % within three months.

review_text:
Ahhh Circus Circus Reno, lets take a tour shall we?  Keep in mind we're not expecting much, this IS Reno, not Vegas.

Lets check in!  Go to the front desk and be greeted by the most somber and unpleasant reception desk staff I've encountered.  Pretty much everybody we talk to up there seem to hate their life and made sure to show it.  What a great vibe to start our trip!

Take a walk through the casino floor on the way to the room.  Take a look around.  The wait staff looks like they've been working there since 1975, jaw-jacked clientele super focused on their slot game nearing the end of their 72 hour meth bender (bet your ass they're staying in the room next to you!).  Outstanding!  Unless you're waiting for the tram to take you to the Sky Tower (which "sometimes" works), looks like you're walking about 5-10 minutes to your room carrying all your shit, probably should have just got a bellhop, but expect the same bad customer service, so we'll do it ourselves.

Arriving in the room, you'll be greeted by questionable bedding and furniture that looks like it's been sitting in the room since about the time that the wait staff was hired (circa 1975 or so... at least the TV was from the late 80s or early 90s).  You might even be lucky enough to find some dirty towels in the bathroom.  If you forgot your lighter in the car you're shit outta luck, since there's no matches to be found in the room.

Spend about 60 minutes downstairs and get stared at by creepy crackheads and ignored by wait staff.  Lets go get a drink at the bar!  Why does the bartender act like I'm unconvincing him be even being there?  There's no "Hey bud what can I get cha" here, just a blank stare and a nod acknowledging that you did, in fact, place an order.

...


Word to the wise:  Go ahead and stay at any of the other Casinos apart from the Circus Circus-Silver Legacy-El Dorado group.  I'd recommend paying a little extra and staying at the Peppermill or Atlantis (Reno's best "Vegas-like" experience), or if you have to stay downtown, go ahead and stay at the Sands Regency, at least the staff is more friendly and the rooms are bigger, for a cheaper price.  Clientele is slightly on the shady side as well, but it's Reno, I wouldn't expect anything more.
issue: The property had a presence of drug‑using individuals and felt unsafe in public areas
fixes: Deploy AI‑enabled CCTV (e.g., Verkada) integrated with real‑time alerting software (e.g., BriefCam) to automatically detect loitering and trigger security dispatch when motion patterns deviate from baseline norms. | - Contract a licensed security firm to provide 24/7 mobile patrols and install RFID badge readers at all entry points; require staff to scan their badges on exit and generate daily compliance reports. | - Implement a tenant‑reporting app (e.g., SafeWatch) that lets residents log suspicious activity instantly, routing submissions to the on‑site security team within 5 minutes and tracking resolution times via a dashboard. | - Conduct monthly vulnerability audits using the SaaS tool iAuditor, assigning corrective actions with due dates and monitoring completion rates against a KPI of ≤10 % unresolved safety items per month. | - Create a “Safe Zone” policy that designates high‑traffic public spaces as drug‑free zones, enforced through QR‑code check‑ins linked to a visitor management system (e.g., Envoy) that records entries and flags repeat violations for automatic tenant alerts.

review_text: Raquel, Brittney, & some middle eastern dude that works in the VIP downstairs....all terrible customer service. As a VIP member I should be receiving better care. They were all rude and unhelpful. I will never stay here again. I can not believe this service. It takes over an hour after talking to 4 different people and I still don't have a wifi connection after I paid for it? Not okay.
issue: Rude and unhelpful staff providing poor customer service, especially for VIP members
fixes: Deploy a real‑time sentiment dashboard (e.g., Medallia Experience Cloud) that flags any VIP interaction scoring below 80 % on “Courtesy” within 24 hours and routes those tickets to a senior manager for immediate follow‑up. | - Launch a mandatory micro‑learning module on empathy and VIP etiquette in the Lessonly LMS, requiring agents to complete a 10‑question quiz with ≥90 % before their next shift. | - Institute a weekly “VIP Service Huddle” where the top‑performing agent from the previous week shares a recorded call (via CallMiner analytics) and leads a 15‑minute role‑play session focused on de‑escalation and premium‑member expectations. | - Implement a tiered incentive program through Bonusly that awards points for achieving ≥95 % positive Net Promoter Score among VIP customers, redeemable for quarterly gift cards. | - Add a post‑interaction NPS survey trigger in Zendesk for every VIP case closure, automatically sending a personalized apology email if the score falls below 7 and logging the response for performance tracking.

issue: Paid Wi‑Fi not working after an hour and multiple contacts
fixes: Deploy an automated Wi‑Fi health check script (e.g., using SolarWinds NPM Wi‑Fi Monitor) that runs every 15 minutes on the guest network and triggers an instant alert in ServiceNow if signal strength drops below 70 % for more than 10 seconds. | - Integrate a self‑service “Wi‑Fi Troubleshooting” portal into the existing Zendesk ticketing flow that presents step‑by‑step guides for resetting devices, checking firewall rules, and verifying login credentials, with a built‑in “Contact Support” button that creates a high‑priority ticket instantly. | - Implement a real‑time Wi‑Fi performance dashboard in Power BI that pulls data from the router’s SNMP logs, flags any network outages longer than five minutes, and sends Slack notifications to the on‑call engineer team for immediate remediation. | - Assign a dedicated “Wi‑Fi Response SLA” of ≤ 30 minutes in ServiceNow, automatically escalating tickets that remain open beyond this threshold to a senior technician and logging compliance metrics in a weekly KPI report. | - Add a post‑interaction survey question (“Did your Wi‑Fi problem get resolved within the promised time?”) to the email follow‑up template, route any negative responses to a quarterly root‑cause review board, and adjust staffing levels based on the resulting Net Promoter Score trend.

review_text:
I work in Reno Feb-March, for the last ....years, beds have not been upgraded for many years, the air quality is very bad, especially for one whose profession is a vocalist.  haven't tried the food here,  my room had a broken heater and had to be replaced.  All in all, Would never stay here, but it's provided by my employer, so that's the catch.  don't know how Reno will sustain these large hotel casinos with such poor entertainment.

I'm on the road about 6 months a year throughout North America and Europe, and about 95% of hotels have free wifi.  Circus Circus charges $10.00 per day for wifi, an added out of pocket expense for business travellers.  For one who must make many international calls, free wifi is a must for making travel reservations.  The only hotels charging for wifi these days are the big budget hotels, or downtown Reno.  Just Sayin', time to change Circus Circus.   Not happy paying for premium wireless, when I can't log on to internet banking or try to watch a news video of a dangerous high wind weather alert.  Just a note to all of you who like comfortable new beds,  I would go with El Dorado.  They have new flat screen TVs, but Circus Circus still has the 1990 model.  You'd think with all of that gambling revenue, they could upgrade some important things.  Hotel premium wireless is $13.00 per day,  download speeds are .31 MBPS,  You might be able to watch a 30 second video in 3 hours at that rate.  Unacceptable.  
On a positive note, some good live music in the Lounge.
issue: Beds are outdated and uncomfortable, not upgraded for years.
fixes: Launch a phased mattress‑upgrade program using a procurement workflow in Coupa: purchase 200 new hybrid mattresses (e.g., Sealy Hybrid) per quarter, track spend against a $500k budget line item, and replace beds in rooms with occupancy ≥ 90% first. | - Integrate GuestU’s post‑stay survey question “Rate bedding comfort” into the mobile check‑in flow; set a target NPS score ≥ 80 on the “bedding” metric within three months and trigger automatic work orders in ServiceNow when scores fall below threshold. | - Deploy a digital asset management system such as Bynder to catalog all existing bed frames, assign a replacement cycle of every two years, and schedule quarterly inspections linked to a KPI dashboard in Power BI that flags any frame exceeding its service life. | - Negotiate a bulk discount contract with a certified supplier (e.g., Sleep Number) and create a “Bed Upgrade ROI Calculator” in Excel that quantifies cost savings from reduced guest complaints and increased ADR, enabling budget approvals for future roll‑outs.

review_text: My fiancé and I will no longer be valued customers as we were not treat with respect. Worst experience ever!!!
issue: Staff were disrespectful and did not treat us with respect
fixes: Deploy a real‑time sentiment monitoring dashboard (e.g., Medallia Experience Cloud) on all front‑line devices that flags negative language triggers and automatically routes the interaction to a supervisor within 5 minutes for immediate coaching. | - Launch a mandatory “Respectful Communication” microlearning module on the company LMS (e.g., Cornerstone OnDemand) with scenario‑based quizzes scored ≥90%; require completion before each shift and track compliance via the LMS reporting API. | - Institute a weekly 15‑minute “Voice‑of‑the‑Customer” huddle where the top three disrespect incidents from the past week are reviewed in a shared Google Sheet, followed by a quick corrective action plan documented and signed off by the team lead. | - Integrate a post‑interaction NPS survey question (“Did you feel respected during your service?”) into Salesforce Service Cloud; set a KPI that any rating ≤6 automatically generates a ticket in Zendesk for a follow‑up investigation and corrective discipline.

review_text:
I give it 2 stars only because it was so darn cheap.  I stayed there for 30 a night. I mean c'mon.  It really doesn't get cheaper than that - even regular motels are more expensive. 

The games there aren't what they used to be when I went as a child and they're so much more pricey.  It's fun to play them again though and win those useless prizes. I actually had fun playing the arcade games where you win tickets and trade them in though.  They still have the old school games that I don't even remember the names of because they're that old.   The shows were so half assed but I wasn't expecting an extravagant show anyway.  It's sad because reno is so empty unless there's an event going on.  The food in that vicinity wasn't that great either and I noticed while and after eating that everyone that was there was 60+.  It's a great meat market for the seniors! haha
issue: The motel is extremely cheap, suggesting low quality and poor value.
fixes: Deploy a price‑benchmarking dashboard in your PMS (e.g., Opera) that pulls weekly competitor rates from sites such as Expedia and Booking.com, then automatically flags rooms priced below market median for manager review. | - Introduce a tiered room classification with standardized amenity bundles (basic, deluxe, suite) and publish transparent pricing matrices on the website using a visual pricing tool like Pricefx, ensuring guests see what they’re getting for their money. | - Launch a “Value Bundle” promotion through the CRM (e.g., HubSpot) that pairs discounted stays with complimentary local experiences (e.g., free breakfast voucher + nearby attraction ticket), tracked by UTM tags to measure conversion lift. | - Install QR‑code‑linked SurveyMonkey micro‑surveys at check‑in and checkout points asking guests to rate perceived value on a 1‑10 scale; set a KPI to achieve ≥8 average score within 90 days and trigger a corrective action plan if missed.

issue: The waiter’s appearance (described as old and ugly) was criticized, affecting perceived professionalism.
fixes: Deploy a quarterly “Style & Grooming” audit using a standardized checklist in Asana, assigning each server a score and requiring a minimum 85 % compliance before shift scheduling. | - Launch a micro‑learning module on LinkedIn Learning titled “Professional Wardrobe Essentials for Service Staff,” with a mandatory 15‑minute quiz that must be passed within two weeks of release. | - Install QR‑code feedback kiosks at each table (via Qualtrics) that prompt guests to rate staff appearance on a 1‑5 scale; aggregate results weekly and trigger corrective coaching when average scores fall below 4.0. | - Create a rotating uniform inventory in Deputy, tracking wear dates and enforcing a replacement cycle of every three months to ensure only current styles are worn by front‑line personnel.

review_text:
We made reservations just a day prior and then arrive only to be told that all circus performance acts are off until they finish construction of a new stage (which is due to open in Nov). This issue here is they made no mention of this on their webpage. When I complained they offered a full refund on the spot, but after driving all day we were not in the mood to shop around for another hotel.
Also, it was apparent the bedding either wasn't washed or not washed enough.
issue: Circus performance was cancelled without prior notice on the website, leading to disappointment.
fixes: Deploy a real‑time cancellation flag in the CMS (e.g., Contentful) that automatically hides all show dates when a “cancelled” status is logged in the ticketing system (e.g., Eventbrite), preventing the public site from displaying unavailable events. | - Configure an automated email/SMS notification workflow in SendGrid that triggers within 15 minutes of any cancellation entry, sending personalized messages with the reason and next‑available date to subscribers who have opted‑in via a Zapier integration. | - Implement a “Cancellation Confirmation” step in the ticketing flow where customers receive a one‑click “Yes/No” prompt before their purchase is finalized; if they select “No,” the transaction is aborted and a refund is issued instantly through Stripe’s Refund API. | - Set up a weekly Power BI dashboard that tracks cancellation rates per venue, flags any event with a >10% cancellation rate, and routes those cases to the operations manager for immediate review and corrective action.

issue: Bedding appeared unwashed or insufficiently cleaned, indicating poor housekeeping.
fixes: Deploy RFID‑enabled linen tags integrated with the hotel’s PMS (e.g., Oracle Opera) so every sheet is scanned at check‑in/out; any tag missed triggers an automatic “missing laundry” alert in the staff dashboard. | - Introduce a digital cleaning checklist on tablets running iAuditor, requiring housekeepers to capture before/after photo evidence of bedding inspection and confirm completion within 5 minutes of room turnover. | - Set up a daily “clean‑room audit” rotation using a mobile app such as HotSOS where supervisors conduct spot‑checks of 10% of rooms, logging compliance scores against a KPI of ≥ 98% clean linens per shift. | - Contract a certified third‑party laundry provider with a Service Level Agreement that mandates delivery of freshly laundered sheets within 24 hours, and track fulfillment via a QR‑code tracking system linked to the property management system.

review_text:
This review is on the kids Midway game area:

As a child, I lived for Circus Circus! My family would come here several times a year and it was amazing and fun.

I took my 9 year old son here this past weekend for the first time and it was garbage to how it was before. They did not at all upgrade this place. They even took out one of the popular games Fantasia! The shows they had going on werent that entertaining. The games were getting overpriced and there wasn't really any new and fun exciting games. My son basically got bored in an hour and really wasn't that entertained by everything. I had to agree with him big time because I knew it was trash compared to what it was before. They need to do a huge upgrade to this place! I'm sure if they did, they would get a lot more visitors.
issue: The kids Midway area has not been upgraded and feels outdated compared to its past condition
fixes: Launch a phased refurbishment plan in Asana, assigning a “Kids Midway Refresh” project with clear milestones (e.g., replace carpet by Q1 2024, repaint walls by Q2 2024) and automatic deadline alerts. | - Install IoT motion‑sensor lighting and temperature controls via Philips Hue Business, programming a schedule that increases illumination during peak kid traffic and reduces energy waste when empty. | - Redesign the play zone using SketchUp models, run a 2‑week virtual mock‑up review with stakeholders, then procure modular, pre‑assembled play units from a vetted supplier (e.g., PlayCore) for installation within a 6‑week window. | - Deploy QR‑code wayfinding linked to a real‑time Google Map overlay on the company’s mobile app, guiding families to the updated Midway area and capturing usage analytics for continuous improvement.

issue: A popular game, Fantasia, was removed from the venue
fixes: Deploy a real‑time “game availability” widget on the website and mobile app (built with React/Next.js) that pulls data from your content management system via API and automatically notifies customers when Fantasia is back online. | - Create a “Fan Request Queue” in Asana where players can submit tickets asking for Fantasia’s return; configure automated email alerts to operations managers and set a SLA of 48 hours for acknowledgment and status updates. | - Run A/B tests in Optimizely to measure impact on churn by comparing conversion rates between venues that retain Fantasia versus those that remove it, then use the results to justify keeping the title on the floor. | - Establish a quarterly “Games Refresh Cycle” documented in Confluence, assigning a cross‑functional team to audit popularity metrics (e.g., PlayStatix usage), renew licensing agreements, and schedule new releases or reinstatements of top‑performing titles such as Fantasia.

issue: The live shows are not entertaining and fail to engage visitors
fixes: Deploy a real‑time audience analytics dashboard (e.g., Qualtrics Experience Management) on stage monitors that displays key engagement metrics (applause rate, dwell time, social mentions) and triggers on‑the‑fly content adjustments based on thresholds. | - Integrate interactive voting via QR codes at seating zones and feed the results into the show’s digital script manager (such as QScout) to instantly add crowd‑preferred segments or surprise acts during breaks. | - Launch a weekly “Show Lab” where performers experiment with new routines in front of a pilot audience, capturing post‑show NPS scores through SurveyMonkey and only rolling out concepts that achieve ≥80 % positive feedback. | - Use AI video analysis tools like IBM Watson Media to automatically flag flat moments in performance and generate automatic “pick‑up” cue cards for the host to insert high‑energy clips when the algorithm detects low energy levels.

issue: The games are priced too high, making them feel overpriced
fixes: Deploy a competitor‑price monitoring tool (e.g., PriceIntelligence) to capture weekly benchmark data; adjust your game prices by no more than 5% above the market median within two weeks of any price shift. | - Implement a tiered pricing model in the storefront (Steam, Epic) using the built‑in discount engine, offering “Lite” versions at 30 % lower than full titles and bundling with DLCs to expand perceived value. | - Run A/B tests on pricing pages via Optimizely, comparing “Standard” vs. “Premium” labels and measuring conversion lift while keeping total revenue constant. | - Launch a loyalty program integrated with your CRM (e.g., HubSpot) that awards points redeemable for future discounts after three purchases, tracking redemption rates to gauge price‑sensitivity impact. | - Conduct quarterly cost‑plus analyses using Excel‑based templates to recalculate COGS and set target margins, then publish revised pricing matrices to finance stakeholders for approval.

fixes: Deploy a “Game Sprint” calendar in Jira Cloud where product managers assign two‑week sprints for rapid prototyping of at least one new title per month, with sprint reviews tracked by story points and user‑acceptance tests. | - Integrate PlayFab analytics to capture daily active users, session length, and churn metrics; set a KPI that 15% of monthly revenue comes from titles launched within the past three months and review the data weekly in Power BI dashboards. | - Launch a quarterly “Kids Game Lab” event using a rotating roster of external indie developers who pitch prototype concepts; select one concept each quarter and provide $10k seed funding plus a dedicated QA sprint on Azure DevOps to fast‑track its release to the app store. | - Implement a “Content Refresh Schedule” in Asana that schedules a minimum of four new game updates (bug fixes, level packs) every 45 days, assigning owners, deadlines, and automatically generating Slack reminders when tasks approach their due dates.
